item.title: Investment firms join Donald Trump’s $100bn race for Venezuelan oil
item.description: Lionheart Capital and Keo Energy create Nasdaq-listed company to pursue assets in Latin American nation.

item.title: Europe’s fighter jet fiasco
item.description: The collapse of Paris and Berlin’s joint venture raises the question of whether either country can do defence alone

item.title: How Wall Street pulled off the biggest IPO in history for SpaceX
item.description: Bankers convinced investors to believe in a sci-fi strategy, overlook steep losses and hand full control to Elon Musk

item.title: UK housebuilder Vistry offers staff voluntary redundancy
item.description: Company is trying to preserve cash to shore up its business amid market slowdown

item.title: The sweet smell of World Cup dollars
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: a dispatch from LA and Mercedes F1’s robust finances

item.title: Goldman and JPMorgan ease office working rules to counter World Cup disruption
item.description: Wall Street lenders say employees can ask to work from home on match days to ease congestion in host cities

item.title: Frasers is better at making headlines than creating shareholder value
item.description: Mike Ashley’s penchant for stake-building is not that well suited to the public markets

item.title: How AI is disrupting investment
item.description: The technology is leading to a fundamental shift in the way investors allocate funds and diversify risks across every asset class

item.title: AI is revolutionising the stock market
item.description: Big Tech no longer prints money; it needs it. What will that mean when confidence dips?

item.title: European tech sovereignty: a doubtful goal but a good investment
item.description: Given the continent’s weak starting point, its modest ambitions could have a big impact on local companies

item.title: Nationalise Heathrow to build third runway, says Emirates boss
item.description: Tim Clark is open to a successor to the discontinued superjumbo A380, even if made in China

item.title: Luke Miels, the quiet GSK boss making a $10.6bn bet on cancer
item.description: The UK pharma group has announced its biggest biotech acquisition in more than 25 years

item.title: Elite sportsman. Entrepreneur. What drives Mathieu Flamini?
item.description: The midfielder who co-founded a biochemicals business on the difference between the locker room and the boardroom

item.title: ‘Rivals’ may be racy — but ITV’s real-life franchise wars were stranger still
item.description: For veterans of Britain’s broadcast licence battles, the Disney+ Jilly Cooper adaptation paints a recognisable picture

item.title: How video games became a channel for political influence
item.description: From China to Saudi Arabia, governments are investing billions in gaming to project influence and assert control

item.title: To bet or not to bet: the corporate prediction market predicament
item.description: Companies are grappling with whether and how to rein in employee wagers that could use confidential information

item.title: Anthropic suspends latest AI models after US blocks access to foreigners
item.description: Trump administration directs company to limit access to foreign nationals on national security grounds

item.title: AI riches turn Samsung factory town into luxury hotspot
item.description: Windfall bonus for workers drives up property prices and department store purchases

item.title: Paramount’s $111bn takeover of Warner Bros wins US antitrust approval
item.description: Deal is critical to David Ellison’s ambition to create new media empire

item.title: Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser becomes dame in King’s birthday honours list
item.description: Barclays and LSE Group bosses miss out despite early-stage approval

item.title: SpaceX’s surge on debut makes Musk world’s first trillionaire
item.description: Rocket and AI group’s shares jumped by nearly a fifth after raising $75bn in record initial public offering

item.title: The strategic dilemmas behind Britain’s defence spending mess
item.description: At the heart of the debate is not just how much the UK should spend on the military, but what types of threat it faces — and ultimately, its place in the world

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO is a display of Musk’s autocratic power
item.description: His unrivalled grip on the public imagination has transmuted into Wall Street gold

item.title: CEO of collapsed Prax ordered fake invoices, administrators allege
item.description: Winston Soosaipillai accused of creating a ‘web of deceit’ before energy group collapsed

item.title: European defence stocks retreat on lower spending and higher wrangling
item.description: Resignation of UK defence secretary shows some governments will struggle to find the money

item.title: Palantir loses legal challenge against Swiss investigative magazine
item.description: Peter Thiel-chaired data intelligence group sued publication over reports that Switzerland rejected its approaches

item.title: Stockpickers: CMC Markets, VP, Motorpoint
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Apollo selects Austin as site of second headquarters
item.description: Private capital giant chose Texas city over Miami and Palm Beach as private schools’ capacity fills up in Florida

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried loses fraud conviction appeal
item.description: Three-judge panel upholds 25-year prison sentence for founder of collapsed crypto exchange FTX

item.title: The Palantir controversy is a block on NHS progress
item.description: Better patient outcomes are at risk in the backlash against American tech
